instruction:
Let the random variable $X \sim U(0, \pi)$, $Y = \sin X$. Use the characteristic function to find $f_{Y}(y)$ is ().

Options:
A. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{1} {\pi} \cdot\frac{2} {\sqrt{1-y^{2}}}, \quad0 < y < 1
$$
B. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{2} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {1-y^{2}}, \quad-1 < y < 1
$$
C. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{3} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {1-y^{2}}, \quad-1 < y < 1
$$
D. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{3} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {\sqrt{1-y^{2}}}, \quad0 < y < 1
$$
E. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{2} {\pi} \cdot\frac{2} {\sqrt{1-y^{2}}}, \quad-1 < y < 1
$$
F. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{1} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {\sqrt{1-y^{3}}}, \quad-1 < y < 1
$$
G. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{2} {e} \cdot\frac{1} {\sqrt{1-y^{2}}}, \quad0 < y < 1
$$
H. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{1} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {\sqrt{1+y^{2}}}, \quad0 < y < 1
$$
I. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{2} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {\sqrt{1-y^{4}}}, \quad-1 < y < 1
$$
J. $$
f_{Y} ( y )=\frac{2} {\pi} \cdot\frac{1} {\sqrt{1-y^{2}}}, \quad0 < y < 1
$$
